cards.back: A Half Adder is a digital circuit that performs the basic addition and subtraction operations. It takes two binary inputs (A and B) and produces three outputs: Sum, Carry Out, and Carry In.
A Full Adder is a digital circuit that performs the basic addition operation with three binary inputs (A, B, and Cin) and produces four outputs: Sum, Carry Out, Carry In, and Carry Out (or Overflow).

cards.front: Verilog HDL implementation of Half Adder
Verilog HDL implementation of Full Adder

cards.back: A Binary Adder-Subtractor is a digital circuit that performs both addition and subtraction operations using binary inputs. It can be implemented using Half Adders and Full Adders.
